claim: Google Gemini's File Search tool provides managed RAG with automatic chunking, embedding, and vector storage—no chunking parameters exposed to users.
actionable: Use Gemini File Search for rapid RAG prototypes when you don't need custom chunking; it handles embeddings and storage automatically.

claim: Gemini File Search supports custom metadata filtering on uploaded documents, allowing queries to be narrowed by user-defined fields (title, URL, etc.).
actionable: When using File Search, attach metadata like title or URL to each document, then filter queries using metadata constraints for precise multi-document retrieval.

claim: File Search responses include grounding metadata with source chunks—this enables building UI features like citation links or timestamp jumps to source material.
actionable: Parse grounding chunks from File Search responses to show users source excerpts and enable click-through links back to original documents or timestamps.

claim: Gemini File Search pricing: embeddings are charged at query time, but vector storage and query-time embeddings are currently free; maximum file size is 100MB per document.
actionable: Use Gemini File Search for cost-sensitive RAG work; only pay for upload-time embeddings, with free storage and query processing (as of Nov 2025).

claim: Unlike NotebookLM, Gemini File Search has a public API and supports programmatic uploads, making it suitable for building multi-document RAG applications.
actionable: Choose Gemini File Search over NotebookLM if you need API-driven RAG; NotebookLM is GUI-only.
